title: A Giant Octopus That Lived with Dinosaurs
summary: A long time ago, about 80 million years, the oceans were home to huge creatures like mosasaurs and plesiosaurs. Scientists thought the biggest hunters were animals with backbones. But a new study found something amazing: a giant octopus that could be up to 19 meters long! Octopuses are soft and rarely leave fossils, but scientists discovered their hard beaks in rocks. They used a special method to grind rocks layer by layer and then used a computer to find the beaks. The beaks were big and had scratches, showing the octopus was a strong hunter. This changes what we know about who was at the top of the food chain back then.

a sea animal with eight arms and a soft body
the hard, pointed mouth part of an octopus
a time period when dinosaurs lived, about 145 to 66 million years ago
an animal that hunts other animals for food

Before this study, what did scientists think were the biggest hunters?

background_read: This article tells us about a new discovery that changes what we know about ancient oceans. Scientists found fossils of a giant octopus that lived with dinosaurs. Octopuses are soft, so their fossils are very rare. The scientists used a clever method to find the beaks inside rocks. This shows that big hunters were not just animals with backbones, but also octopuses.
Article_Structure: WHO: Scientists (Yasuhiro Iba and his team) studied a giant octopus called Nanaimoteuthis haggarti.
WHAT: A giant octopus that lived 80 million years ago was discovered. It could be up to 19 meters long.
WHERE: The fossils were found in rocks from the Cretaceous period.
WHY: The study shows that octopuses were top predators in the ocean, changing what scientists thought.

perspectives.description: Scientists thought the biggest hunters were vertebrates like mosasaurs.
Scientists now know that giant octopuses were also top predators.
